title: New Base Evaluation Service
summary: Over the years of running contests and helping builders, Easter Bunny and I have noticed something interesting. A lot of builders aren’t here to compete, they’re here to learn. And, they'd like to see how their base stacks up, where it shines, and where it can grow. So we built a system specifically for that. It's not about competition and whose base is "best". It's looking at a base with an evaluation based on the continuum of skills and techniques the community has evidenced, and letting you k

summary: this is probably really simple and my brain just doesn't process correctly. I'm making a small cyberpunk type club setting for my base. I'm trying to do neon and disco type lights. I've achieved a nice neon glow with simple base lighting settings but now I'm looking for the type of things you'd expect at a club, like flashing disco lights, etc. I can't seem to find anything in the base items that gives me what I want.

summary: So I finally bit the bullet and transferred servers, which meant I lost my hard work and time that I put into my base on Torchbearer. The problem is: I want to create a new base where I am for myself, and well, the game crashes every few seconds of even attempting to edit anything. What has happened since I've been gone? I don't see any topics on the crashing so I can only assume it's a me thing, or possibly a settings issue. Can anyone help? I can barely create a single room let alone select a

title: Base Builders Cheat Sheet
summary: Thanks for the Base Love, Leo! quote author=Leandro The Cheat Sheet: Keys: Shift = Hold for vertical movement Ctrl = Hold for horizontal movement Alt = Hold for spin Shift + Alt or Ctrl = hold for 360 spin F1 = Cycle through grid sizes F2 = Cycle through snap angle degrees F3 = Enable or Disable room clipping F5 = Cycle through anchor points (floor, wall, ceiling, surface) Shift + Tab = Cycle through visible items Ctrl + Z = Undo The Slash commands: /edit_base [1/0] = Turn base editing on or off

summary: I feel like I'm going mad. I thought the massive hydraulic phwooshing and clanking noise playing nonstop in my base was from the 5th column pumps, but I've removed them and re-logged and it's STILL MAKING THE NOISE. It's so loud... It has to be one of the sewer pieces, right? I cannot find it for the life of me. Something in the walls.... Has anyone had this problem before? Does anyone know what's making that sound?

title: Another Way To Organize Your Ios
summary: Here is my take on organizing my IOs in a more "cleaner" style. Basic version: 2 Legacy Terminals 1 Enhancement table (tech) The first terminal is placed against a wall (you need to hide the enhancement table. The second terminal is flipped upside down and mostly hidden in the wall.  This gives a nice tech backing to whatever wall it is against. The enhancement table is flipped upside down with the pedestal just showing through.  The actual base also shows through and gives you a nice area to pl

title: Battling Placement Limitations
summary: You have to place items inside of room bounds, and those boundaries extend vertically, but did you know  that, under some conditions, you can also push them out a little, horizontally? When making a space ship, you can turn the outside of the base into part of the ship, so you don't have to hide the base? Here's how!

title: Community Challenge Contests
summary: All About Community Challenges These contests are meant to be short and low stress. We hope that people will challenge themselves and have fun with this! If it’s something you’re not sure how to do, there are people you can ask for help, and we hope you’ll give it a shot even if you’re not sure. Concept: Community Challenges will be short contests with one winner Challenges will be created by members of the community Challenges are judged by the CRs and the Challenger Winner will get a title and

title: Community Choice Winner
summary: We, your Community Representatives and the judging panel, would like to thank this community very much for helping us bring this community choice vote to its conclusion. We didn’t really plan to put in the week of extra work it’s taken to do this right, but we’re base builders: when do we ever take the easy way? When doing things right is important, you do what you have to do. And there were problems with the first round of voting. We wanted to make sure that, whomever won, there was no question

title: Base Contest Winners
summary: BASE CONTEST RESULTS! The results are in for the Second Homecoming Base Contest! We had 74 bases that were entered into 6 categories. These are the finalists and the winners! UTILITY BASES These bases offer services. They may or may not have a story, but should be decorated in a consistent and thematic way, but without consideration for making sure characters can sit or access things as if it was a real situation. title: Community Choice Award Announcement With New Information
summary: We would like to heartily thank all the participants in the Second Homecoming Base Contest, which was our first as Community Representatives! title: Base Crawl
summary: The Base CR's, Easter Bunny and Dacy, are holding a base crawl for the contest! What's a base crawl, you ask? We will be visiting each of the bases in the Community Choice contest together, talking about the bases and making sure we point out every area you can access, so you don't miss any, answering questions, until we've visited 5 of them on each weekday session. Then we'll visit all of them on Saturday's session in one big crawl! After each crawl, we will have a trivia contest about the base
